title: Important Precautions for Protecting Spiritual Life | Sri Amitasana Dasa | SB 4.19.9 | 06.11.2025
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, canto number 4, chapter number 19, text number 9
Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, canto number 19, text number 19, text number 19
Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shri Prabhupada
Translation
King Pruthu was presented with various gifts from the general populace and predominating deities of all planets.
The ocean and seas were full of valuable jewels and pearls, and the hills were full of chemicals and fertilizers.
Four kinds of edibles were produced profusely.
As stated in the Isopanishad, this material creation is supplied with all the potencies for the production of all necessities required by the living entities,
not only human beings, but animals, reptiles, aquatic species.
The ocean and seas produce pearls, coral, and valuable jewels so that fortunate law-abiding people can utilize them.
Similarly, the hills are full of chemicals so that when rivers flow down from them, the chemicals spread over the fields to fertilize the four kinds of foodstuff.
They are technically known as charviya, those edibles which are chewed,
lehiya, those which are licked up,
chhusiya, those which are swallowed,
and piya, those which are drunk.
Pruthu Maharaj was greeted by the residents of other planets and their presiding deities.
They presented various gifts to the king and acknowledged him as the proper type of king by whose planning and activities everyone throughout the universe could be happy and prosperous.
It is clearly indicated in these words that the oceans and seas are meant for producing jewels,
but in Kali Yuga, the oceans are mainly being utilized for fishing.
Shudras and poor men were allowed to fish, but the higher classes like the Kshatriyas and Vaishyas would gather pearls, jewels, and coral.
All the poor men would catch tons of fish. They would not be equal in value to the piece of coral or pearl.
In this age, so many factories for the manufacture of fertilizers have been opened,
but when the personality of Godhead is pleased by the performance of yagnas,
the hills automatically produce fertilizing chemicals, which help produce edibles in the field.
Everything is dependent on Godhead.
Everything is dependent on the people's acceptance of the Vedic principle of sacrifice.
Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta.
There was a devotee of Krishna who was in company of the Lord.
He was walking with the Lord and then in course of the walk he asked the Lord,
Lord Shri Krishna,
I have heard about your divine energy Maya.
Can you please explain what is this divine energy and how it influences the living entity?
Why it is called Dhrittaya?
Generally devotees who are especially pure devotees,
they are out of the purview of Maya.
So the Lord said, well, this is a big subject.
In fact, the entire material world is nothing but play of Maya.
The living entity wants to enjoy and the whole world,
whatever is happening is the illusory energy of the Lord captivating the living entities.
So it's a long story.
So anyway, the Lord told that devotee,
let's sit and talk whenever we have some long discussion.
So we say, okay, let's sit and talk.
It's going to take time.
So they were, they sat down on one rock and then Krishna,
because it's going to be a long story,
long description.
So Krishna said, before we start,
can you give me a, get me a glass of water?
So, yes, my Lord.
So the devotee immediately went in search for water.
It was open land, hot summer day.
And then he walked for a while.
He could not see anywhere where he could get water.
Kept walking, kept walking, kept walking.
And then fortunately at a distance,
he could see some greenery, some cottages,
some kind of a village.
So at last he was very happy that for sure here I will get water.
And then I'll go back.
And then I listened from the Lord.

About Maya.
So he went to that village and then he saw one cottage from a distance,
went to that cottage, knocked on the door and the door opened.
That very beautiful, charming lady opened the door.

a guest was considered as an Athito Devobhava.

Okay, eat something before you leave.

He felt like, let me continue my talking.
Let me stay for some more time.

And they continued talking, interacting.
Again, 100x, he developed affection and love for her and proposed to her.

100x, they got children.
100x, the children got married.

The children were very nice.
And the grandchildren were very, very cute.
And more so when the grandchildren would call him Daddu, Daddu.
You know, it was so sweet to hear loving, affectionate voice.
And he would also very affectionately keep them on his lap, play with them and all this.
So years rolled by.
And he got very, very attached to the family.
Fortunately, the family was very good, very loving, very kind, very affectionate.
And then one day it so happened, suddenly, you know, it became very cloudy and thunder in the sky.
And it started raining and raining very profusely, very heavily.
So much so that everywhere was water.
Water got clogged.
And so much so that it started flooding.
The whole village started, the water started rising and all this.
And then because water was gushing from the nearby villages also, there was some kind of a very strong force in the water.
And then, you know, they all started getting panicky.
And, you know, children, grandchildren, and they started saving and, you know, trying to, the cattle, the lamb, whatever there in the home,
they were trying to safeguard whatever the grains were kept.
And then soon the water kept on rising.
And so much of current, they started seeing that many of the things started flowing away with the water.
And they were all desperately trying to save themselves, save everything what they had, the valuables.
And in front of their eyes, one by one, one by one, everything started flowing with the water.
So much so that the more they tried, the more they tried.
So much so that the more they tried.
The more they tried to hold on to things, but right in front of their eyes, everything was getting wiped away.
And then he, with his wife, held each other and then children.
But in front of his eyes, some of his grandchildren got swept away.
He's trying to save them.
Krishna finally, you know, because he was a devotee of Krishna, he said, he's desperate, desperate.
Generally when a person goes through tough times, we remember Krishna.
So he said, Krishna, Krishna, please, Krishna, please help.
Finally, in front of his eyes, everything got swept away.
And then finally, you know, pleading the Lord, please, please save me, save me.
He fell at the feet of the Lord.
Save me, Govinda.
And then he hears a tap.
He opens his eyes.
Krishna says, where is the glass of water?
And he was seated there, that very place where Krishna had asked him a glass of water.
So this is the illusory energy of the Lord, which all of us in this material world are influenced by.
And in the illusion, under the illusion of the Lord, there is an illusion.
Under the influence of this illusionary energy, we forget who we are.
Forget the goal of life.
Forget our relationship with the Lord.
Forget that the goal of life is to act for pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord.
To perform yagyas, what is being mentioned here.
And when yagyas are performed, Prabhupada in the Prabhupada is explaining,
how there is a profuse supply of everything.
Even in the Gita, Krishna says,
when yagyas are performed, there will be sufficient rains.
Then there will be enough grains.
There won't be any shortage.
And people can live peacefully.
In today's world, there is no yagna being performed.
Because, forget about performing yagna.
People have lost, because of Maya,
don't even know that we are amsa of the Lord.
We have to please the Lord.
We have to serve the Lord.
And everyone is engaged in serving their own senses,
serving their own bodies,
and relatives, friends, family members.
This is Maya.
And Bhagavatam time and again is saying that,
what is that world in which people act.
So, Prithu Maharaj, what we see in the description,
how there was profuse supply of rain.
It was enough, when the rains would fall from the hills,
all the chemicals will come down.
And because the land was fertile,
there was enough grains.
There was no shortage of anything.
So, Prabhupada towards the end of this purport is explaining,
that how everything is dependent on people's acceptance
of the Vedic principles of sacrifice.
Yagna does not mean necessarily that fire yagna what we do.
That's why Sankirtana Yagna,
there is no fire.
General, typical when you talk about people,
we have to do Yagna.
So, Yagna what comes in their mind is that fire sacrifice.
But Yagna has a broader understanding.
Yagna means any activity,
which is done for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord.
Any activity.
Even the service what we do is a Yagna.
Even chanting what we do is a Yagna.
Yagnair Sankirtana Prayer.
So, there was once a devotee who came to Shila Prabhupada.
And said that my wife is very unhappy with me and she is quarrelling.
So, Prabhupada said, why?
She said that she does not want me to do management service.
She wants me to do more of Pujari,
Deity worship, cooking, Deity related services.
She does not want me to do management services.

He said, your wife is less intelligent.
She does not know what is Yagna.

And then he gives example.
Just like Arjuna was fighting in the battlefield and he was the greatest yogi.

you know, this service is Deity service or Pujari service or some services which have,
which quote unquote we get a feeling that they are very spiritual in nature.
And there are some services which are not.

So, Prabhupada gave us a very broader and a holistic understanding of Krishna Consciousness.
He said, in Kali Yuga, for propagating this Krishna Consciousness movement,
there will be lot of managerial work.
But then he says, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu promises that,
if we are doing this managerial work, we will not be entangled.
So, Yagna, which here we are talking about is not just, you know, doing fire Yagna or doing Sankirtana Yagna.
It is broader understanding.
All different forms of services which we are doing for the pleasure of the Lord is a Yagna.
Management also is a Yagna.
Sometimes we have to do services which are very, very full of anxieties there.
Prabhupada talks about spiritual anxiety.
Many times we want to be in a comfort zone.
We want to do only things which are very pleasing to the body, pleasing to the mind.
Arjuna, what he was doing? He was fighting.
There was bloodshed.
And bloodshed with whom? His near and dear ones.
His elders, his respectable, worshipable elders.
But because it was for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord, it was Yagna.
If we do for our pleasure of our own mind and senses, it is sense gratification.
Even apparently it may be a spiritual service.
For what we are doing? What is the intent?
What is the motive behind our action?
If the motive is pleasure of Guru and Krishna,
then even apparently some service which involves lot of dealings with materialistic people is Yagna.

And when we are under the influence of Maya,
automatically our actions will be governed by what is pleasing to mind and senses.

Prabhupada is going to come next day.

Not so much because we are weak in sadhana.

Krishna Prabhupada is very merciful.

It is tantamount to Vaishnava Pradha and then our consciousness is ruined.

We will have, you know, we will be Krishna conscious.
Krishna conscious, which is ultimately what we are trying to be.
